title: Wire grid polarizer with silane protective coating
abstract: A wire grid polarizer (WGP) (10) can have a conformal-coating (13) to protect the WGP (10) from at least one of the following: corrosion, dust, and damage due to tensile forces in a liquid on the WGP (10). The conformal-coating (13) can include a silane conformal-coating with chemical formula (1), chemical formula (2), or combinations thereof: (Formula (1)) A method of applying a conformal-coating (13) over a WGP (10) can include exposing the WGP (10) to Si(R 1 ) d (R 2 ) e (R 3 ) g . In the above WGP (10) and method, X can be a bond to the ribs (12); each R 1 can be a hydrophobic group; each R 3 , if any, can be any chemical element or group; d can be 1, 2, or 3, e can be 1, 2, or 3, g can be 0, 1, or 2, and d+e+g = 4; R 2 can be a silane-reactive-group; and each R 6 can be an alkyl group, an aryl group, or combinations thereof.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is : 1. A wire grid polarizer (WGP) comprising : a. ribs located over a surface of a transparent substrate, wherein the ribs are elongated and arranged in a substantially parallel array; b. gaps between at least a portion of the ribs; and c. a silane conformal-coating located over the ribs, wherein the silane conformal-coating includes chemical formula ( 1), chemical formula (2), or combinations thereof: where : r is a positive integer; each R 1 independently is a hydrophobic group; X is a bond to the ribs; each R 3 is independently selected from the group consisting of: a silane-reactive-group, -H, R 1 , R 6 , X, and combinations thereof; iv. each silane-reactive-group is independently selected from the group consisting of: -CI, -OR 6 , -OCOR 6 , -N(R 6 ) 2 ,-OH, and combinations thereof; and v. each R 6 is independently an alkyl group, an aryl group, or combinations thereof. The WGP of claim 1, further comprising a phosphonate conformal-coating located over the ribs, wherein the phosphonate conformal-coating includes 

 O II R 1 â Pâ R 5 I z where : a. Z is a bond to the ribs; b. R 5 is a phosphonate-reactive-group, R 1 , R 6 , or Z; and c. the phosphonate-reactive-group is -CI, -OR 6 , -OCOR 6 , or -OH. 3. The WGP of claim 2, wherein : a. each of the ribs includes a rod and a wire; b. the wire is reflective; c. the rod is absorptive; d . X is a bond to the rod; and e. Z is a bond to the wire. 4. The WGP of claim 3, wherein : a. X is -O-Si ; and b. Z is -O-Metal, where Metal is a metal atom. 5. The WGP of claim 1, wherein R 3 is -OCH 3 . 6. The WGP of claim 1, wherein the hydrophobic group includes a carbon chain. 7. The WGP of claim 1, wherein each hydrophobic group is independently CF 3 (CF2)n(CH2)m, where n and m are integers within the boundaries of: 4 < n < 10 and 2 < m < 5. 8. The WGP of claim 1, further comprising a silicon dioxide conformal-coating, wherein the silicon dioxide conformal-coating : a. is located between the silane conformal-coating and the ribs; and b. has a thickness of between 0.1 nanometers and 5 nanometers. A method of making a wire grid polarizer (WGP) with a protective coating, the method comprising : a. providing a WGP with ribs located over a surface of a transparent substrate, wherein the ribs are elongated and arranged in a substantially parallel array; b. exposing the WGP to a silane chemical by vapor deposition, the silane chemical including Si(R 1 )d(R 2 )e( 3 ) g where: i. d is 1, 2, or 3, e is 1, 2, or 3, g is 0, 1, or 2, and d+e+g = 4; vi. each R 1 is independently a hydrophobic group; ii. R 2 is a silane-reactive-group; iii. each silane-reactive-group is independently selected from : -CI, - OR 6 , -OCOR 6 , -N(R 6 ) 2 , and -OH ; iv. each R 6 is independently an alkyl group, an aryl group, or combinations thereof; v. each R 3 , if any, is independently any chemical element or group. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising exposing the WGP to a phosphonate chemical by vapor deposition, the phosphonate chemical including (R 1 ) i PO(R 4 )j(R 5 )k, where : a. i is 1 or 2, j is 1 or 2, k is 0 or 1, and i+j+k = 3; b. R 4 is a phosphonate-reactive-group; c. each phosphonate-reactive-group is independently selected from -OR 6 , -OCOR 6 , and -OH; d. each R 5 , if any, is independently any chemical element or group.
